name: Pork Medallions with Port Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Slice the tenderloin crosswise into medallions 1/2 inch thick. Sprinkle both sides with salt and pepper, and then dredge each in flour to coat, shaking off any excess.
2. Heat the butter and oil in a heavy skillet over medium-high heat. Saute the medallions in small batches for about 1 minute per side, turning only once. Remove each batch to an ovenproof serving dish and keep warm in a 225°F (107°C) oven.
3. Prepare the sauce: Place the skillet with meat drippings back on high heat. Add chicken stock and wine. Bring to a boil, scraping and stirring until some of the broth evaporates. Continue cooking the sauce until it thickens enough to coat the back of a spoon, about 6-8 minutes.
4. Serve the meat with the sauce spooned over each medallion. Per 4 ounce serving: 347 calories
